title: Greg Pikitis

jokes.text: Greg Pikitis. You're the Parks lady, right? Yeah, that's right. I'm the Parks lady.
jokes.setup: Greg dismissively calls Leslie 'the Parks lady'
jokes.punchline: Leslie owns it with pride instead of being offended
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - turns potential insult into badge of honor

jokes.text: He's like an invisible, adolescent, James Bond super villain criminal mastermind.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to ridiculous spy thriller terms
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-dramatization - turns teen pranks into espionage thriller

jokes.text: Or maybe someone else is doing it, but I really feel like it's this kid.
jokes.setup: After building up Greg as criminal mastermind
jokes.punchline: Leslie admits uncertainty with casual 'but I really feel like'
jokes.explanation: Great deflation after the build-up - shows Leslie's tunnel vision

jokes.text: Thanks for stopping by, Leslie. You look great. Thank you. Ends today.
jokes.setup: Greg politely compliments Leslie after her threats
jokes.punchline: Leslie accepts compliment then continues threatening
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward politeness mixed with menace - very character-specific

jokes.text: Hey, slutty teenage girls dressed as sexy kittens, pump your own stomachs this year.
jokes.punchline: Darkly specific reference to teen drinking consequences
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected dark humor from nurse Ann - professional knowledge meets party planning

jokes.text: Who's not invited, then? Hey! What's going on, cupcake?
jokes.punchline: April immediately appears, creating awkward timing
jokes.explanation: Well-timed cringe moment - classic sitcom embarrassment

jokes.text: Can't wait to see how tiny your costume is.
jokes.setup: April's deadpan response to being uninvited
jokes.punchline: Backhanded comment about Ann's presumably revealing costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect April snark - cutting but delivered with characteristic deadpan

jokes.text: That looks like something you would find on the wall of a serial killer. In a way, that's a compliment.
jokes.setup: Dave compares Leslie's stalking chart to serial killer evidence
jokes.punchline: Leslie takes it as a compliment about her dedication
jokes.explanation: Excellent character beat - Leslie's obsession reframed as positive trait

jokes.text: But more importantly, Greg Pikitis sucks, and I wanna destroy him.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining practical reasons for stopping Greg
jokes.punchline: Admits her real motivation is personal vendetta
jokes.explanation: Pure Leslie honesty - drops pretense for petty personal motivation

jokes.text: Didn't, like, 30 people die in that fire? He wasn't Superman.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising mayor for saving recipe during deadly fire
jokes.punchline: Leslie casually dismisses 30 deaths with 'He wasn't Superman'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and quotable - Leslie's warped priorities perfectly captured

jokes.text: He looks like Ron Swanson. Is that who this is based on? No. It's based on William Percy. Were you listening to what I just said?
jokes.punchline: Leslie's frustrated response to Andy's obvious non-listening
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy obliviousness meets Leslie exasperation

jokes.text: A week ago I had nothing. Now, I have a part-time job. I'm gonna make $32 tonight. I owe Leslie everything I have. Which, after tonight, will be $39.
jokes.setup: Andy expressing gratitude for his new job
jokes.punchline: Reveals pathetically small amounts and math error
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - genuine gratitude mixed with mathematical incompetence

jokes.text: You're a doctor. Yup. I meant your costume. I got it.
jokes.punchline: Doctor's flat 'I got it' shows Ron's failed attempt at humor
jokes.explanation: Ron's rare attempt at small talk falling flat - character-appropriate awkwardness

jokes.text: Worlds colliding, always awkward.
jokes.punchline: Ann's self-aware commentary on the situation
jokes.explanation: Generic observation - not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: And she made out with me in my cop car, which is pretty neat. I shouldn't have said that. That's clear to me now.
jokes.setup: Dave sharing intimate detail in interview
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret and self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good talking-head moment - oversharing followed by immediate regret

jokes.text: I did. I'm a straight person.
jokes.setup: Ann saying it's okay Derek didn't wear costume
jokes.punchline: Derek reveals his 'costume' is being straight
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unexpected and clever - subverts costume expectations perfectly

jokes.text: Last year, I saw three Jonas Brothers make out with three Robert Pattinsons. It was amazing.
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd image of pop culture icons making out
jokes.explanation: Fun absurd image but relies on pop culture references without much twist

jokes.text: So, you kind of dropped the ball with Andy then, didn't you?
jokes.punchline: Bluntly criticizes Mark for Andy situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron directness creating social discomfort

jokes.text: Turdish? Yes. Like a turd. Like a little turd.
jokes.punchline: Leslie doubles down with childish repetition
jokes.explanation: Leslie's petty vocabulary expansion - childish but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Pikitis!
jokes.punchline: Dramatic villain-name shout to empty room
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic villain confrontation trope - Leslie's over-the-top response

jokes.text: This is his ace of spades. This is his calling card. This is what he leaves all his victims.
jokes.punchline: Treating fruit pit like serial killer signature
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to earlier serial killer comparison - Leslie's conspiracy thinking

jokes.text: You're not. Hey. Yeah, I'm gonna leave. Oh. Okay. This isn't that fun.
jokes.setup: April can't drink because she's underage
jokes.punchline: Bluntly announces party isn't fun and leaves
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - brutal honesty with zero social filter

jokes.text: I'm gonna take this home. Nobody was drinking it, so I'm gonna take it. Okay. Gonna take this, too. Been meaning to read it.
jokes.punchline: Casually steals multiple items including a book
jokes.explanation: April's casual kleptomania escalating from drink to book

jokes.text: You have a scary face. Hey! No, in a good way. Scary cute.
jokes.setup: Leslie tells Dave he has a scary face
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to make it a compliment
jokes.explanation: Leslie's social awkwardness - trying to recover from insult

jokes.text: This isn't one-way glass, you know. I can see you.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to secretly communicate with Dave
jokes.punchline: Greg pointing out obvious fact about regular window
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Greg breaking fourth wall of interrogation scene

jokes.text: I screw with her 'cause she gets all riled up, and her face gets all scrunched up like this.
jokes.setup: Greg explaining why he messes with Leslie
jokes.punchline: Makes exaggerated impression of Leslie's angry face
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Greg doing Leslie impression - plays on their dynamic

jokes.text: And almonds give me the squirts.
jokes.punchline: TMI about digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry overshare - character-appropriate but low-hanging fruit humor

jokes.text: My name is Bert Macklin. I'm with the FBI.
jokes.punchline: Uses completely made-up FBI identity
jokes.explanation: Iconic Andy moment - birth of Bert Macklin character, perfectly absurd

jokes.text: This is not a stripper. It's my wife, Wendy. You remember her? She's a surgeon at County General, and she's super hot.
jokes.punchline: Over-defensive explanation mixing respect with objectification
jokes.explanation: Tom's insecurity about his hot wife - character-driven cringe

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Who'd be boring enough to do that?
jokes.setup: Ann thanking Wendy for not dressing as doctor
jokes.punchline: Wendy's response while Ann is literally dressed as book character
jokes.explanation: Ironic response given Ann's literary costume choice

jokes.text: Okay. The gloves are coming off.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration for questioning a teenager
jokes.explanation: Leslie's over-dramatic escalation for minor situation

jokes.text: Never mind that I was actually eating a plum when you saw me earlier.
jokes.punchline: Greg reveals it was wrong fruit entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Leslie's 'evidence' - smart kid vs. obsessed adult

jokes.text: If you followed me all night then you know that it wasn't me.
jokes.punchline: Greg points out this proves his innocence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic trap - Greg uses Leslie's obsession against her

jokes.text: Look, I will waterboard you. No, no. I don't think she would make a good cop.
jokes.punchline: Dave's concerned professional assessment
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation met with professional concern - great character contrast

jokes.text: Ron Swanson's in the building, y'all!
jokes.punchline: Announces Ron like he's a celebrity DJ
jokes.explanation: Absurd hype for the most anti-party person in the cast

jokes.text: Bert Macklin, FBI.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Pikitis asking who they are
jokes.punchline: Andy still maintaining FBI cover
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Bert Macklin introduction - Andy's commitment to the bit

jokes.text: The President of the United States of America.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Pikitis asking who to call about Leslie
jokes.punchline: Andy suggests calling the President
jokes.explanation: Andy's absurd escalation of authority - perfect character logic

jokes.text: Yeah, it was bad FBI work, too. My bad.
jokes.setup: Dave apologizing for bad police work
jokes.punchline: Andy apologizes for equally fake FBI work
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback maintaining the FBI bit with casual apology

jokes.text: Tonight, the 'T' in T-Pain stands for Tom Haverford!
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to rebrand famous rapper's name
jokes.explanation: Tom's try-hard DJ persona - character-appropriate but forced pun

jokes.text: Yeah, but you were probably a nerd, though, huh? Hey. Leslie, I mean that in a good way.
jokes.setup: Andy saying Leslie didn't do stupid stuff in high school
jokes.punchline: Calls her a nerd then tries to backtrack
jokes.explanation: Andy's social clumsiness - well-intentioned but poorly executed

jokes.text: What? No. I'm dating Ann. Good. That was a test. You passed.
jokes.punchline: Claims it was a test after rejection
jokes.explanation: Standard 'it was a test' deflection - not particularly original

jokes.text: I'd hate for you to have to go back to Canada. All that socialized medicine up there.
jokes.setup: Ron expressing sympathy for Wendy's situation
jokes.punchline: Lists socialized medicine as negative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron libertarian moment - deadpan political humor

jokes.text: This is for pooping on the handball court!
jokes.setup: Leslie listing Greg's crimes while TPing
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly absurd vandalism
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity in Leslie's revenge list - unexpectedly gross

jokes.text: This is really fun! But I don't condone it!
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous enjoyment and moral disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Leslie's moral flexibility when it serves her - perfect character contradiction

jokes.text: About to head home and have crazy sex. That's cool. I don't... Yeah!
jokes.punchline: Mark's uncomfortable non-response
jokes.explanation: Standard TMI cringe humor - not particularly clever

jokes.text: Did he hire a fake mom again, to get him out of trouble? What? Whenever he gets in trouble, he goes on Craigslist and hires a woman to play his mother and bail him out.
jokes.setup: Leslie confused by Greg's mom appearing
jokes.punchline: Reveals Greg's elaborate fake parent scheme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Greg's schemes - completely unexpected and perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Oh, that little SOB! Greg! Gregory!
jokes.setup: Learning about Greg's fake mom scheme
jokes.punchline: Real mom's immediate fury
jokes.explanation: Payoff to fake mom revelation - real mom's authentic anger

jokes.text: I'm not even in the FBI, stupid.
jokes.punchline: Andy breaks character to insult Greg
jokes.explanation: Perfect end to Bert Macklin bit - Andy breaks character in most Andy way possible

jokes.text: How did that little turd do it?
jokes.setup: Leslie still puzzled by Greg's method
jokes.punchline: Ends episode with 'turd' callback
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier 'turd' vocabulary - Leslie's obsession continues

tmdb_overview: Leslie enlists the help of Dave and Andy in order to catch a local teen vandal. Meanwhile, Ann struggles to make her Halloween party fun, and ends up getting help from an unlikely source.
insight: Halloween prank obsession escalates absurdly; 49 jokes in 24 minutes fuels Season 2's momentum shift.
